Homeowners comparing cleaning services want to see pricing upfront. "Call for a quote" loses to the competitor who shows "$120 for a 2BR/1BA." Transparency builds trust.
Zero location-specific pages. When someone Googles "house cleaning Miami", Nekoblue Services is invisible. Each service area page captures local searches.
Move-in/move-out cleanings are high-ticket ($300-$600) one-time jobs. Without a dedicated page, searches for "move out cleaning Miami" go to competitors.
No "100% satisfaction guarantee" or "we'll come back" promise. This is standard in cleaning and removes the risk for first-time customers.
Deep cleaning is a high-ticket service ($250-$500). Without a dedicated page, "deep cleaning Miami" searches go to competitors.
Google can't understand the business type, service area, or hours from the code. Schema markup helps show rich results in local search.
The meta description controls what people see in Google search results. Without one, Google picks random text from the page.
No blog means no long-tail SEO. Competitors writing "How much does house cleaning cost in Miami?" capture searches this site never will.
No GA4, no GTM — the business is flying blind. No way to know which pages work or what's generating calls.
Airbnb turnover cleaning is a growing high-frequency revenue stream. Without a dedicated page, this market segment goes to competitors.

impactEstimate.bounceReason: 3.7s load time (53% leave after 3s) + phone not clickable on mobile + no contact form
impactEstimate.installNote: If even 3 of those became recurring weekly clients, that's another $2,400-$3,600/month.

Spring cleaning season is here — search volume for house cleaning spikes 40% in spring. Every website gap is costing maximum leads right now.

businessSummary.diagnosticDescription: The Cleaning Crew Charleston has some elements right (secure site with https redirect, reviews displayed on site (custom)) but 6 high-impact gaps are holding it back.

gapSummary.impact: We couldn't fully analyze contact forms, booking widgets, and CTAs. Manual review recommended.
We couldn't fully analyze meta tags, schema, and SEO elements. Manual review recommended.

gapSummary.gap: Phone number not clickable on mobile
gapSummary.impact: 73% of cleaning searches happen on mobile. Visitors have to manually type the number. Most will tap the back button and call the competitor who makes it one tap.

Cleaning is an in-home service. Homeowners need to feel safe letting cleaners into their house. Without "bonded", "insured", or "background-checked" messaging, trust drops immediately. Competitors who show this win.

gapSummary.gap: No online booking or instant quote
gapSummary.impact: No ZenMaid, BookingKoala, or any booking widget. No bedroom/bathroom price calculator. Visitors expect instant pricing for cleaning services — without it, many abandon before contacting. Spring cleaning season is here — search volume for house cleaning spikes 40% in spring. Every website gap is costing maximum leads right now.

gapSummary.gap: No clear CTA above the fold
gapSummary.impact: Visitors land on the page and don't know what to do next. No "Book a Cleaning", "Get Instant Quote", or "Schedule Online" visible without scrolling.
Only a phone number. Many homeowners prefer to submit a request rather than call — especially for scheduling regular cleaning.

No mention of weekly, biweekly, or monthly plans. Recurring customers are the lifeblood of a cleaning business ($4,500 LTV vs $180 one-time). The site should frame recurring as the default, not an afterthought.
Homeowners comparing cleaning services want to see pricing upfront. "Call for a quote" loses to the competitor who shows "$120 for a 2BR/1BA." Transparency builds trust.
Google can't understand the business type, service area, or hours from the code. Schema markup helps show rich results in local search.
The meta description controls what people see in Google search results. Without one, Google picks random text from the page.
No GA4, no GTM — the business is flying blind. No way to know which pages work or what's generating calls.
Airbnb turnover cleaning is a growing high-frequency revenue stream. Without a dedicated page, this market segment goes to competitors.
Footer says "© 2023". Small but signals neglect. 2 minutes to fix.
Not in WebP/AVIF format. Converting them cuts page size and improves load time.
Most images lack alt text. Hurts SEO and accessibility.
No mention of green, non-toxic, or eco-friendly products. Families with kids and pets specifically search for this. It's a key differentiator.
No visual proof of cleaning results. Before/after photos are powerful conversion tools for visual services like cleaning.
No introductory discount or first-clean special. First-time offers reduce hesitation and get new customers in the door. Competitors using "$20 off your first clean" win more trials.

gapSummary.gap: Missing viewport meta tag — not mobile-optimized
gapSummary.impact: No viewport meta tag means the site renders at desktop width on mobile. Google's mobile-first indexing penalizes this.
No canonical URL specified. This can cause duplicate content issues if the site is accessible via multiple URLs (www vs non-www, HTTP vs HTTPS).
Missing the primary heading tag. The H1 tells Google the main topic of the page. Without it, the page lacks semantic structure.
With 234+ reviews, existing customers clearly like the service. A referral program turns happy customers into a lead generation engine.
